title: json_extract_string_field: hand-rolled scanner must not be replaced with serde_json
description: The hand-rolled JSON scanner in secretx-core exists because serde_json deserializes all field values into plain (non-Zeroizing) String allocations. Switching to serde_json would leave every non-target secret field lingering in heap memory until the allocator reuses those pages — defeating the zeroization guarantee that SecretValue provides. The scanner is correct (RFC 8259 compliant, handles surrogate pairs, validates all escape sequences including in skipped fields), well-tested, and carries a prominent DO NOT REPLACE comment. A future contributor might propose 'simplifying' this to serde_json; the correct response is to point them at the comment and the zeroization design in CLAUDE.md.
